briefing.opening: Good morning, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 9, 2026. Based on the 66 climate-related news items collected today, we will comprehensively highlight the severity of the accelerating climate crisis, domestic and international policy and technological efforts to address it, and the importance of citizen participation.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Climate Crisis, Increasing Severity Domestically and Internationally
sections.content: Recent research has for the first time confirmed that global warming has accelerated over the past decade [43, 53, 56, 61], and an analysis indicates that the impacts of climate change are far more severe than previously feared [38]. Domestically, extreme weather phenomena are intensifying, with heatwaves increasing 2.2 times and tropical nights 42 times compared to 1910 [6], and in Gangwon Province, complex disasters such as droughts following heatwaves are rapidly increasing [12]. Consequently, Australia's Murray-Darling Basin is facing a sustainability crisis [2], cocoa production areas in Colombia may shift to higher altitudes [41], and lobster catches in Maine are declining due to climate change and rising costs [44, 46], illustrating severe economic and ecological threats globally. Furthermore, climate change threatens the safety of modern buildings [59], and insurers are expected to bear costs of 492 million euros in 2025 [62], projecting immense financial burdens across society [57].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Climate Adaptation Policies and Legislative Efforts
sections.content: The necessity of enacting a 'Climate Adaptation Act' to proactively respond to the accelerating climate crisis and protect vulnerable populations is being strongly raised domestically [3, 9]. Discussions on the legislation of climate adaptation policies are actively underway, including the successful hosting of a 'Legislative Task Force Debate on the Enactment of the Climate Crisis Adaptation Act' organized by Assemblywoman Jo Ji-yeon [8, 11]. Furthermore, as climate change has been identified as a hidden threat to industrial accidents [4], the Ministry of Labor has also promised to bring about changes that citizens can feel. Discussions on cooperation to respond to the climate crisis have also taken place in the agricultural sector [10], and the benefits of physical activity initiatives for climate change mitigation and adaptation, as well as integrated climate change strategies for healthy communities, are gaining international attention [39, 65].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Transition and Carbon Neutrality Technology Development
sections.content: As decarbonized energy transition is emphasized as the answer in the era of climate crisis [23], various efforts are continuing domestically and internationally to expand renewable energy supply and achieve carbon neutrality. Korea Water Resources Corporation (K-water) is accelerating the concrete implementation and expansion of water and renewable energy cooperation following the Korea-Philippines summit [22, 25, 27, 28, 30, 31, 33], and the Seoul Metropolitan Area Air Quality Management Office has also conducted information sharing activities to expand renewable energy supply [34]. Domestically, the chemical and nuclear industries are collaborating for carbon neutrality using 'High-Temperature Gas-cooled Reactor' technology [17], and in Gokseong County, the 'Sunshine Income Village Promotion Team' has been launched for the first time in Jeollanam-do, drawing attention to efforts to link solar power profits to residents' income [36]. In the United States, renewable energy is projected to dominate the power grid by 2026 [26], and the evolution of renewable energy management through AI technology is also anticipated [29]. However, the Korean new and renewable energy industry stands at a crossroads of structural transformation between the speed of carbon neutrality and securing power stability [19], and continuous policy support is needed as the Ministry of Climate is seeking solutions to the issue of renewable energy output restrictions [24]. Additionally, a warning has been raised that the U.S. Critical Minerals Club could threaten a fair clean energy transition [51].

sections.title: 🌍 Food Security and Ecosystem Adaptation in the Era of Climate Crisis
sections.content: Agricultural and ecosystem adaptation efforts are underway worldwide to prepare for an era of food insecurity due to climate change. In Hwasun County, Jeollanam-do, education for stable production of high-quality rice in response to climate change has been successfully completed [5], and Incheon City provides citizens with opportunities to become urban farmers by cultivating container gardens [37]. Internationally, research is being conducted on how perennial crop agriculture can contribute to solving food security and social division issues [40], and cacti are gaining attention as future food and fuel sources in the era of climate change [47]. At the ecosystem level, a biodiversity and ecosystem conservation agreement has been reached in the Yellow Sea region to respond to the climate crisis [13], and the Amazon rainforest is showing signs of self-adaptation, such as producing more young leaves with high photosynthetic efficiency to regenerate its canopy amidst climate change [52, 55].

sections.title: 📰 Promoting Climate Action Through Citizen Participation and Awareness Raising
sections.content: The importance of citizen participation and awareness-raising for responding to the climate crisis is growing. The Seoul Energy Dream Center is expanding citizen-participatory environmental education by renovating its interactive exhibition space, the 'Carbon Neutrality Experience Center' [14, 15, 16, 18]. Internationally, the 'Green Lens Awards' have been launched to honor film and TV projects addressing sustainability and climate change [45], and student works are being used to advocate climate change messages [64], indicating active movements encouraging climate action in the cultural and arts sectors. Furthermore, the participation of diverse professions and social strata is being emphasized, with new research indicating that hairdressers can play a significant role in climate change discussions [58]. While critical perspectives on climate change reporting methods are also being raised [63], discussions are also continuing on ways to mobilize the 'silent majority' for climate action [60].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing reconfirmed the severity of accelerating climate change, while also demonstrating that multifaceted efforts in policy, technology, and citizen participation are underway to address it. Climate adaptation and the transition to carbon neutrality are not options but necessities, and the active participation and interest of all of us will be a crucial step towards a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
